meta.reference: Gilboy N, Tanabe T, Travers D, Rosenau AM. Emergency Severity Index (ESI): A Triage Tool for Emergency Department Care, Version 4.
meta.note: Decision tree evaluated top-down. First matching level is assigned.

esi_1_criteria.description: Immediate life-saving intervention required. Unresponsive or critical vital-sign derangement.

consciousness.description: AVPU = Unresponsive triggers ESI-1 regardless of vitals.

critical_vitals.description: Any single critical vital triggers ESI-1.

esi_2_criteria.description: High-risk situation, altered consciousness (not Unresponsive), or severe distress. Should not wait to be seen.

altered_consciousness.description: AVPU = Pain or Voice triggers ESI-2 (Unresponsive already captured by ESI-1).

vitals_danger_zone.description: Safety-net thresholds at the ESI-2/3 boundary. If a patient would otherwise be ESI-3 but has ANY danger-zone vital, upgrade to ESI-2.

severe_pain.description: Severe pain or distress (NRS >= 7/10) in a high-risk context upgrades to ESI-2.

esi_3_to_5_resource_logic.description: For patients not meeting ESI-1 or ESI-2 criteria, the expected number of resources determines the level. Resources = labs, imaging, IV fluids/meds, procedures, specialist consults.

esi_3.description: Expects 2 or more resources (e.g., labs + imaging, or labs + IV + consult).

esi_4.description: Expects exactly 1 resource (e.g., X-ray only, or wound suture only).

esi_5.description: Expects zero resources. Prescription refill, simple reassessment, minor complaint.

level_mapping.description: Maps ESI levels to approximate RETTS equivalents for cross-engine comparison.
